---
title: "Enabling Containerd For The Container"
---

---
title: "Enabling containerd for the Container"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Content-Federation-Services/cvp1770396777005"
section: "Content Federation Services"
---

The containerd program needs to be enabled in Docker to run the OCI container for the CF Broker.

For a computer using Windows, the setting Use containerd for pulling and storing images is available from the Docker desktop application:

![The Docker desktop setting "Use containerd for pulling and storing images" set to Enabled.](data:image/gif;base64,)
For a computer using Linux, you need to add the setting "containerd-snapshotter": true to the daemon.json file.

To enable containerd in Linux:

1.  Navigate to the docker program folder.
2.  Open the daemon.json file in a plain text editor.
3.  Add the following feature configuration:
```
{
  "features": {
    "containerd-snapshotter": true
  }
}
```

4.  Save the daemon.json file.
5.  Restart the Docker engine, which can be done using the following command:
```
sudo systemctl restart docker
```
